IS this short circuit no longer true?
Instead of doing a short-circuit there, I'm earl-returning in the enable-blinking function further down below. This prevents the creation of the timer in the first place.
📝 this was some load-bearing code in the past. make sure it still works
There won't be a renderer instance in ConPTY at all anymore. Regular conhost does have a renderer of course, but it doesn't need to be cleaned up on exit (the OS will handle that for us).

These tests seem like they'd still be valuable, no? We'd need to trim out all the bits that are checking the literal output, but it seems like we should still have a mechanism for testing that the `Terminal` is in the right state given doing {{thing}} to the console host
I've written brand new tests in `VtIoTests.cpp`. They assert that calling a console API a certain way produces a given VT output.

Sure, but like, this file has like 50 examples of weird wacky edge cases of combining a bunch of calls, and making sure that the host and terminal are in agreement. I'd hate to lose all those tests - some for particularly gnarly bugs. Especially the resizing ones.

Unfortunately, almost all tests assert the current VT-to-VT translation. Since we pretty much don't modify VT output anymore, most tests cannot possibly apply anymore. 😣

Here are the ones I found that we can port:
* TestResizeHeight
  ❔ I'm not entirely sure how to translate the test though, as it asserts that VtEngine produces a given output on resize. This PR however never emits any output on resize by design, so what output would we test?
* ResizeRepaintVimExeBuffer
  ❔ Similar to above, we don't emit any output on resize anymore, so I'm not sure how to port this.
* ClsAndClearHostClearsScrollbackTest
  ✖️ Partially covered by `VtIoTests::ScrollConsoleScreenBufferW`.
  ✅ I forgot to test this in `VtIoTests::FillConsoleOutputCharacterW`.
* ClearBufferSignal
  ✅ This one should now assert that no output is produced.

<details>
<summary>These are the ones we cannot port over</summary>

* ConptyOutputTestCanary
  ❌ Unit test for VtEngine.
* SimpleWriteOutputTest
  ❌ Unit test for VtEngine.
* WriteTwoLinesUsesNewline
  ❌ Tests if a text-CUP-text sequence is translated to text-CRLF-text. With VT passthrough we won't do that anymore.
* WriteAFewSimpleLines
  ✖️ Covered by `VtIoTests::WriteConsoleW` (which should be extended to cover more inputs to be fair)
* PassthroughClearScrollback
  ❌ Tests if CSI 3 J is passed through.
* PassthroughClearAll
  ❌ Tests if CSI 2 J is passed through.
* PassthroughHardReset
  ❌ Tests if ESC c is passed through.
* PassthroughCursorShapeImmediately
  ❌ Tests if DECSCUSR is passed through.
* PassthroughDECCTR
  ❌ Tests if DECCTR is passed through.
* TestWrappingALongString
  ✖️ Covered by `ScreenBufferTests::MultilineWrap` among others.
* TestAdvancedWrapping
  ❌ Unit test for VtEngine.
* TestExactWrappingWithoutSpaces
  ✖️ Covered by `VtIoTests::WriteConsoleW`.
* TestExactWrappingWithSpaces
  ✖️ Covered by `VtIoTests::WriteConsoleW`.
* MoveCursorAtEOL
  ❌ Tests if a CUP + CSI K is translated to space + CUP. With VT passthrough we won't do that anymore.
* OutputWrappedLinesAtTopOfBuffer
  ❌ Unit test for VtEngine & covered by `ScreenBufferTests::MultilineWrap` among others.
* OutputWrappedLinesAtBottomOfBuffer
  ❌ Unit test for VtEngine.
* ScrollWithChangesInMiddle
  ❌ Unit test for VtEngine (a combination of the previous 2 tests).
* DontWrapMoveCursorInSingleFrame
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* ClearHostTrickeryTest
  ❌ Unit test for VtEngine (alternate screen buffer, etc.).
* OverstrikeAtBottomOfBuffer
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* MarginsWithStatusLine
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* OutputWrappedLineWithSpace
  ❌ Unit test for VtEngine (ensures that a space can wrap a line).
* OutputWrappedLineWithSpaceAtBottomOfBuffer
  ❌ Unit test for VtEngine (ensures that a space can wrap a line).
* BreakLinesOnCursorMovement
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* ScrollWithMargins
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* TestCursorInDeferredEOLPositionOnNewLineWithSpaces
  ❌ Asserts behavior of VT sequences (cannot break with VT passthrough).
* TestResizeWithCookedRead
  ✖️ Asserts that cooked read doesn't crash.
* NewLinesAtBottomWithBackground
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* WrapNewLineAtBottom
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* WrapNewLineAtBottomLikeMSYS
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* DeleteWrappedWord
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* HyperlinkIdConsistency
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* ResizeInitializeBufferWithDefaultAttrs
  ✖️ I think this is covered by `ScreenBufferTests::TestReflowEndOfLineColor`.
* SimpleAltBufferTest
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* AltBufferToAltBufferTest
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* TestPowerLineFirstFrame
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* AltBufferResizeCrash
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* TestNoExtendedAttrsOptimization
  ❌ Unit test for VtEngine.
* TestNoBackgroundAttrsOptimization
  ❌ Unit test for VtEngine.
* SimplePromptRegions
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* MultilinePromptRegions
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* ManyMultilinePromptsWithTrailingSpaces
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* ReflowPromptRegions
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.
* ClearMarksTest
  ❌ Tests if a mix of VT is passed through.

</details>
